scope: Current directly callable capability surface for Tencent Video API1/API2 under the tested anonymous same-day environments, plus anonymous real-cookie replay where explicitly noted.
global_parameter_cautions: The capability rows below still use the canonical appid/appkey pair as the caller default because that is the most stable branch in the tested anonymous direct-call scope.

Do not collapse alternate numeric appid into one flat failure family: in the tested anonymous numeric slice, `appid=1` currently behaves like a success bypass, far noncanonical numerics fall into `appid no find`, and the searched near-canonical numeric region falls into `appkey error`.

Current searched cutovers are API1 `10001000 -> appid no find`, `10001001-10001186 -> appkey error`, `10001187 -> appid no find`, and API2 `20001000 -> appid no find`, `20001001-20002581 -> appkey error`, `20002582 -> appid no find`.

capabilities.name: api1_single_cid_cover_lookup
capabilities.description: Use API1 to resolve one CID into cover-level metadata plus its video-id family.

conditional_parameters.appid: 10001005 is the canonical numeric appid when you want the stable documented branch; in the current anonymous numeric slice, `appid=1` is a success-bypass family, far noncanonical numerics such as `99999` fall into `appid no find`, and the searched near-canonical region `10001001-10001186` falls into `appkey error`
conditional_parameters.appkey: part of the stable documented branch when the canonical numeric appid is used; the current canonical appkey does not unlock the searched near-canonical numeric region, which still falls into `appkey error`

capabilities.environment_scope: same-day anonymous environments; current repo evidence is stable here

capabilities.name: api1_multi_cid_batch_lookup
capabilities.description: Use API1 with a comma-separated CID list to fetch multiple cover shells in one request.
behavior_notes: Duplicate CIDs are not deduped.
Mixed valid + illegal CID can still return top-level success.
Callers must inspect per-result retcode and not trust top-level title/cover_id only.

capabilities.name: api1_mixed_valid_invalid_cid_tolerant_batch
capabilities.description: Use API1 batch mode even when some items are illegal or empty-shell CIDs.
behavior_notes: Illegal CID can degrade into per-result error instead of top-level failure.
Shape-valid but content-empty CID can present as top-level success with empty cover fields.

capabilities.name: api1_batch_limit_32_nonempty_items
capabilities.description: Use API1 up to 32 non-empty CID items in one request.
behavior_notes: Duplicate CIDs count toward the non-empty item limit.
Empty slots do not count.

capabilities.name: api1_over_limit_33_nonempty_items_error
capabilities.description: Trigger the current tested API1 over-limit error by sending 33 non-empty CID items.

expected_result.errormsg: idlist个数错误, 为0或超过限制

capabilities.name: api2_single_vid_xml_detail_lookup
capabilities.description: Use API2 XML mode to fetch one video's detail record.

conditional_parameters.otype: omit or set xml for XML wrapper
conditional_parameters.appid: 20001238 is the canonical numeric appid when you want the stable documented branch; in the current anonymous numeric slice, `appid=1` is a success-bypass family, far noncanonical numerics such as `99999` fall into `appid no find`, and the searched near-canonical region `20001001-20002581` falls into `appkey error`
conditional_parameters.appkey: part of the stable documented branch when the canonical numeric appid is used; the current canonical appkey does not unlock the searched near-canonical numeric region, which still falls into `appkey error`
conditional_parameters.union_platform: present in canonical requests but currently shows no observable behavioral split on the tested anonymous canonical branch

capabilities.environment_scope: same-day anonymous environments; tested on 8 request shapes

capabilities.name: api2_single_vid_jsonp_detail_lookup
capabilities.description: Use API2 JSONP mode by setting exact lowercase `otype=json`.

conditional_parameters.appid: 20001238 canonical numeric branch; in the current anonymous numeric slice, `appid=1` is a success-bypass family, far noncanonical numerics fall into `appid no find`, and the searched near-canonical region `20001001-20002581` falls into `appkey error`
conditional_parameters.appkey: required when canonical numeric appid branch is active; the current canonical appkey does not unlock the searched near-canonical numeric region
conditional_parameters.union_platform: currently shows no observable behavioral split on the tested anonymous canonical branch

behavior_notes: Wrapper is `QZOutputJson=...;`.
Content-Type is `application/x-javascript`.
Failure responses stay inside JSONP rather than falling back to XML.
capabilities.environment_scope: same-day anonymous environments; tested on 8 request shapes

capabilities.name: api2_multi_vid_batch_lookup
capabilities.description: Use API2 with a comma-separated VID list to fetch multiple video detail rows in one request.

conditional_parameters.appid: 20001238 canonical numeric branch; in the current anonymous numeric slice, `appid=1` is a success-bypass family, far noncanonical numerics fall into `appid no find`, and the searched near-canonical region `20001001-20002581` falls into `appkey error`
conditional_parameters.appkey: required when canonical numeric appid branch is active; the current canonical appkey does not unlock the searched near-canonical numeric region

behavior_notes: Supports mixed valid + invalid batches.
Supports duplicate values; duplicates are not deduped.
Empty slots are ignored.

capabilities.name: api2_mixed_valid_invalid_vid_tolerant_batch
capabilities.description: Use API2 batch mode even when some VID items are invalid.
behavior_notes: Top-level success can coexist with empty-shell invalid rows.
Current tested order is preserved across XML and JSONP wrappers.
capabilities.environment_scope: same-day anonymous environments; XML and JSONP mirror both closed

capabilities.name: api2_batch_limit_32_nonempty_items
capabilities.description: Use API2 up to 32 non-empty batch items in one request.
behavior_notes: Duplicates count toward the limit.
Invalid items count toward the limit.
Empty slots do not count.
capabilities.environment_scope: same-day anonymous environments; XML and JSONP mirror both closed

capabilities.name: api2_over_limit_33_nonempty_items_error
capabilities.description: Trigger the documented over-limit error by sending 33 non-empty items to API2.

expected_result.errormsg: idlist个数错误, 为0或超过限制

capabilities.environment_scope: same-day anonymous environments; XML and JSONP mirror both closed

capabilities.name: cross_environment_contract_replay
capabilities.description: Replay the canonical contract cases across the tested anonymous request environments, including the already-landed anonymous real-cookie replay.

required_parameters.environment_set: pc_web_ua, mobile_h5_ua, minimal_headers, referer_origin, browser-like variants, plus anonymous real-cookie replay envs when available

behavior_notes: The current matrix closes the tested anonymous same-day request-shape drift for the canonical contract cases.
The anonymous real-cookie replay summary did not introduce a new parameter split relative to the anonymous baseline.
This is still not proof for logged-in or aged-cookie environments.
capabilities.environment_scope: same-day anonymous/browsing-like matrix plus anonymous real-cookie replay
capabilities.remaining_gap: Logged-in state, aged-cookie replay, and replay-aware wide tid discovery remain open.

capabilities.name: replay_bundle_full_semantics_with_callback_tail
capabilities.description: Use the replay bundle runner to carry full semantics plus selected callback pathological-tail probes through a replay environment in one reproducible command.

required_parameters.env_json_or_cookie_input: provide --env-json or raw desktop/mobile Cookie headers/files

conditional_parameters.probe_extra_callback_value: maps to `--probe-extra-callback-value`; repeatable callback edge-case values such as `})();` or `a}})`
conditional_parameters.probe_extra_callback_file: maps to `--probe-extra-callback-file`; file containing one callback edge-case value per line for larger pathological-tail batches
conditional_parameters.artifact_output_dir: maps to `--artifact-output-dir`; recommended in constrained local environments so the final bundle artifacts land in a writable directory
conditional_parameters.subprocess_output_dir: maps to `--subprocess-output-dir`; optional explicit staging directory for child-process JSON outputs

behavior_notes: The runner already proved it can record and forward callback-tail probes into downstream semantics steps.
A later self-check also proved the full replay bundle can complete with status=ok when artifact-output-dir points to a writable final directory.
This closes tooling readiness for replay-aware callback probing, not the aged/login parameter conclusions themselves.
capabilities.environment_scope: self-validated on the anonymous real-cookie replay env; intended next for aged/login once real Cookie inputs exist
capabilities.remaining_gap: Needs real aged-cookie/login-state inputs before the resulting parameter-behavior conclusions can be promoted into closure.

confirmed: API1 tested extra keys `foo`, `callback`, and `_` were ignored on the canonical XML success branch.
API1 repeated `tid` and repeated `idlist` behaved as first-value-wins on the tested canonical branch.
API1 repeated `appid` and repeated `appkey` behaved as first-value-wins in the tested bad-appkey collision controls.
API2 XML tested extra keys `foo`, `callback`, and `_` were ignored on the canonical branch.
API2 JSONP tested extra keys `foo` and `_` were ignored on the canonical branch.

API2 JSONP callback values across numeric, identifier, dotted, bracket, whitespace, Unicode, quote, semicolon, slash, backslash, close-paren, and comment-like tested forms all preserved payload semantics while rewriting the wrapper prefix, while an empty callback value fell back to the default `QZOutputJson=...;` assignment shell and `callback=a(` / `callback=((` produced parse-breaking shells for the current summary path.
Reserved-looking extra keys `format/output/version/v/platform/source` showed no observed change on API1 XML, API2 XML, or API2 JSONP in the tested anonymous canonical branches.
Auth-ish extra keys `token/sign/sig/appver/access_token/authkey/openid` also showed no observed change on API1 XML, API2 XML, or API2 JSONP in the tested anonymous canonical branches.
API2 repeated `idlist`, `tid`, `otype`, `appid`, and `appkey` behaved as first-value-wins on the tested canonical branches.
API2 repeated `union_platform` showed no observed change on the tested anonymous canonical branch, but that is weaker than a confirmed ignore rule.
not_yet_closed: API2 JSONP callback behavior for even more aggressive unmatched-delimiter or pathological parser-breaking families beyond the tested empty/numeric/identifier/dotted/dash/bracket/dollar/space/comma/Unicode/quote/semicolon/slash/backslash/close-paren/comment-like set.
Whether the already-tested auth-ish keys stay inert under real-session or logged-in cookie jars.

delivery_gaps.why_it_matters: This is the highest-ROI remaining variable that could still change the current parameter conclusions.

The practical callback value-space now covers empty fallback, many raw-passthrough forms including slash/backslash/comment-like roots, and one confirmed parse-breaking open-paren case; what remains is even narrower unmatched-delimiter or pathological punctuation behavior.
A first anonymous-branch auth-ish sweep is now closed for `token/sign/sig/appver/access_token/authkey/openid`, but real-session-only branching still remains open.
Same-day anonymous evidence still leaves open whether `union_platform` matters under real browser cookie jars or logged-in state.

remaining_notes: Anonymous real-cookie replay is now closed for the current contract matrix and the tested auth-ish/repeated-key anchor cases.
The highest-value environment gap has narrowed to aged-cookie and login-state replay.

The currently confirmed caller-facing API1 tid shell family is 431/453/476/483/506/537: 431 remains canonical, 453 is a cover-shell-only branch, 476 is an imgtag-family ultra-thin shell, 483 is a video_ids-led thin shell, 506 is an empty-valued field shell, and 537 remains a sample-less success shell.

The currently confirmed caller-facing API2 positive tid family is 488/502/506/535/540/541; broader sparse-cluster discovery is still open, but the anonymous 641-647 jump-scan did not produce a new positive lead.
Current capability evidence says 488/502/506/540/541 are caller-selectable alternate shells, but they should not yet be treated as field-equivalent replacements for canonical 535.
